description: Swarm Discussion Mode — structured multi-role debate with code-enforced round protocol, session reuse, consensus voting, and human-readable discussion tracking UI.

userStories.title: Discussion.Round schema adds max_rounds + internal lock refactor
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Discussion tracker to store max_rounds and use atomic write locks to prevent race conditions.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/board/discussion.ts, add max_rounds: z.number() to the Round schema
Change Discussion.start() signature to start(swarm, channel, workers, max_rounds) and store max_rounds in the Round object
Refactor load/save into internal loadRaw/saveRaw functions that do NOT acquire locks
Change record() to use a single write lock for the entire load-modify-save cycle: using _ = await Lock.write(key(swarm, channel)), then loadRaw, modify, saveRaw
Change join(), advance(), and any other mutating functions to also use write lock wrapping loadRaw/saveRaw
Existing read-only functions (status, tally) keep read locks unchanged
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Two changes in one story because they both touch the same internal lock structure. loadRaw/saveRaw are private helpers; public API unchanged except start() gains max_rounds param.

userStories.title: Discussion.join() — dynamic participant registration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a function to register a Worker's role_name into Discussion.expected[] after the discussion has started.
acceptanceCriteria: Add async function join(swarm: string, channel: string, role: string): Promise<Round> to Discussion namespace in src/board/discussion.ts
join() acquires write lock, calls loadRaw, adds role to expected[] if not already present, calls saveRaw, returns updated Round
If no discussion exists for the channel, throw an error with message including channel name
Duplicate role names are silently ignored (idempotent)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This fixes the critical bug where expected[] is always empty. Called from delegate_task when discussion_channel is present.

userStories.title: delegate_task passes role_name and calls Discussion.join()
userStories.description: As a developer, I need delegate_task to accept a role_name parameter and register the Worker in the Discussion tracker.
acceptanceCriteria: Add role_name: z.string().optional() to the delegateParameters schema in src/tool/delegate-task.ts, with description 'Role name for discussion mode (e.g., PM, RD, QA)'

In the Swarm worker registration block (after info.workers.push and Swarm.save), add: if discussion_channel AND role_name are both set, import Discussion and call Discussion.join(swarm_id, discussion_channel, role_name)
The join call is fire-and-forget with .catch(e => log.warn('discussion join failed', { error: e }))
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: role_name is the Discussion system's unique key. Conductor passes it when creating each Worker.

userStories.title: Swarm.discuss() uses { name, perspective } roles schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Swarm.discuss() to accept roles with name and perspective fields, and pass max_rounds to Discussion.start().
acceptanceCriteria: Change Swarm.discuss() input.roles type from Array<{ agent: string; perspective: string }> to Array<{ name: string; perspective: string }>
Update the goal message construction: list roles as '{i}. Role: "{name}" — Perspective: {perspective}'

Include explicit instructions in the goal for Conductor: 'When calling delegate_task for each role, set role_name="{name}" and discussion_channel="{channel}". All Workers use the default sisyphus agent.'
Change Discussion.start() call from start(id, channel, []) to start(id, channel, [], max_rounds) where max_rounds is input.max_rounds ?? 3
Update SwarmDiscussTool in src/tool/swarm.ts parameters to match: roles as z.array(z.object({ name: z.string(), perspective: z.string() }))
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Removes the agent field from roles. Every Worker is sisyphus; the only difference is the perspective prompt and role_name.

userStories.title: Monitor round complete message includes structured round info
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Monitor's round complete message to include round/max_rounds/is_final so Conductor has deterministic information.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/session/swarm.ts monitor(), when roundState.complete is true, include roundState.max_rounds in the formatted message
Format: '[Discussion Round {round} Complete — round {round}/{max_rounds}{, FINAL if round >= max_rounds}]'

After the opinions list, append actionable guidance: if not final → 'Next action: call board_write advance_round, then prompt each role for round {round+1}.', if final → 'All rounds exhausted. Make final decision and post decision artifact.'
For consensus signals in the final round, also include tally results in the message
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This removes the need for Conductor to remember which round it's on. The Monitor tells it explicitly.

userStories.title: board_write advance_round and board_read discussion operations
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Conductor to advance rounds and read discussion state through board tools.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/tool/board.ts, import Discussion from '../board/discussion'
Update BoardWriteTool operation enum to: z.enum(['create_task', 'update_task', 'post_artifact', 'signal', 'advance_round'])
Add advance_round handler: extract channel from params.data.channel, call Discussion.advance(swarm_id, channel), return Round as JSON with title 'Advanced to round {N}'
Update BoardReadTool operation enum to: z.enum(['tasks', 'artifacts', 'signals', 'snapshot', 'discussion'])
Add discussion handler: extract channel from params.filter.channel, call BoardSignal.thread(swarm_id, channel) and Discussion.status(swarm_id, channel) in parallel, return { round, thread } as JSON
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Two operations in one story because they share the Discussion import and are both small additions to existing tool handlers.

userStories.title: Rewrite conductor-discussion.txt with session reuse protocol
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Conductor discussion prompt to teach the precise multi-round protocol using session_id reuse, run_in_background, role_name, and Monitor-driven advancement.
acceptanceCriteria: Rewrite src/agent/prompt/conductor-discussion.txt

Round 1 section: (1) create discuss task with channel, (2) for each role call delegate_task with role_name=<name>, discussion_channel=<channel>, run_in_background=true, prompt includes: 'Your role name is <name>. Your perspective: <perspective>. Read channel <channel> via board_read. Then post a proposal signal via board_write. The signal from field MUST be <name>, payload must contain { round: 1, summary: your proposal }.', (3) record each returned session_id, (4) STOP AND WAIT for system message '[Discussion Round 1 Complete]'

Round 2+ section: (1) on receiving Round Complete message, call board_write advance_round with channel, (2) for each role call delegate_task with session_id=<saved>, run_in_background=true, prompt references specific points from round summary, instructs to post opinion/objection signal with correct from and round, (3) WAIT for Round Complete

Final round section: (1) on receiving 'FINAL ROUND NEXT', advance round, (2) prompt each role to post consensus signal with { position: agree/disagree/modify, summary: reasoning }, (3) wait for Complete + tally, (4) unanimous → decision artifact + complete, (5) not unanimous → Conductor decides, documenting dissent
Prompt under 1200 tokens
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The key behavioral guarantees: (a) Workers always use role_name in from field, (b) Conductor WAITS for Monitor, never advances on its own, (c) session_id reuse for continuity.

userStories.title: User entry points: REST route and TUI command for discuss
userStories.description: As a developer, I want HTTP and TUI entry points for launching discussions.
acceptanceCriteria: Add POST /swarm/discuss route to src/server/routes/swarm.ts BEFORE the GET /:id route
Body schema: { topic: z.string(), roles: z.array(z.object({ name: z.string(), perspective: z.string() })), max_rounds: z.number().optional(), config: SwarmConfig.optional() }
Handler calls Swarm.discuss(body) and returns Swarm.Info
Use describeRoute with operationId 'swarm.discuss'

Update src/command/template/swarm.txt: add 'discuss' subcommand that uses swarm_discuss tool with topic text, defaults to roles [{ name: 'PM', perspective: 'Focus on user value, scope control, and product-market fit' }, { name: 'RD', perspective: 'Focus on implementation feasibility, performance, and technical debt' }, { name: 'QA', perspective: 'Focus on edge cases, error handling, testing strategy, and security' }]
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: POST /swarm/discuss must be before /:id to avoid Hono treating 'discuss' as an id parameter.

userStories.title: GET /swarm/:id/discussion REST endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a REST endpoint that returns a structured, human-readable discussion summary so the Web UI and intervene workflows have context.
acceptanceCriteria: Add GET /swarm/:id/discussion route to src/server/routes/swarm.ts
Find the discuss task for this swarm: BoardTask.list(id).then(tasks => tasks.find(t => t.type === 'discuss')). If none found, return { topic: null }
Extract channel from the discuss task metadata (task.metadata.channel or task.scope[0] — use whatever Conductor stores when creating the discuss task)
Call Discussion.status(id, channel) for round info and BoardSignal.thread(id, channel) for the thread

Build response: { topic: task.subject, channel, round: { current: round.round, max: round.max_rounds, complete: round.complete }, participants: round.expected.map(name => ({ name, spoken: round.received.includes(name) })), thread: signals.map(s => ({ round: s.payload.round, from: s.from, type: s.type, summary: s.payload.summary })), decision: find last artifact of type 'decision' for this swarm and return its content, or null }
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This endpoint powers the Discussion Thread UI component and gives users context when they want to intervene.

userStories.title: Web Dashboard — Discussion Thread panel
userStories.description: As a user, I want a dedicated discussion thread view on the Swarm Dashboard when the Swarm is in discussion mode.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/app/src/pages/swarm/discussion-thread.tsx component
Fetch discussion data from GET /swarm/:id/discussion on mount, refetch on SSE events
Display header: topic text, round progress 'Round {current}/{max}', participant status chips (name + ✓ if spoken, ⏳ if not)

Display thread grouped by round: each round has a heading 'Round {N}: {Proposals|Responses|Consensus}', under which each signal shows as a card with role name badge (colored by type: proposal=blue, opinion=gray, objection=orange, consensus=green), signal type label, and full summary text
For participants who have not spoken in the current round, show a placeholder card with ⏳ and role name
At the bottom, if decision is not null, show a highlighted decision card with ✅ icon, decision text, and tally (agree/disagree/modify counts)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Replaces the generic ActivityFeed + TaskGraph layout when the Swarm has a discuss task. The ActivityFeed still shows non-discussion events below.

userStories.title: Swarm Dashboard integrates Discussion Thread panel
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Swarm Dashboard to show the Discussion Thread panel when the Swarm contains a discuss task.
acceptanceCriteria: In packages/app/src/pages/swarm/swarm-dashboard.tsx, detect discussion mode: check if swarm data has any task with type 'discuss' (fetch tasks from /swarm/:id or include in swarm response)
When discussion mode is detected: replace the main content grid (TaskGraph + AttentionQueue + ActivityFeed) with the DiscussionThread component as the primary panel
Keep the Worker cards row above the Discussion Thread (Workers still show their status)
Keep Pause/Resume/Stop controls and message input (intervene) in the header — intervene lets users influence the Conductor during discussion
When NOT in discussion mode: keep existing layout unchanged (TaskGraph + ActivityFeed)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Conditional rendering based on task type. The Discussion Thread component handles its own data fetching and SSE updates.

userStories.title: ActivityFeed discussion signal formatting
userStories.description: As a user, I want the ActivityFeed to format discussion signals with role labels and colors when they appear alongside other events.
acceptanceCriteria: In packages/app/src/pages/swarm/activity-feed.tsx, in the onmessage handler, detect discussion signal types: proposal, opinion, objection, consensus
For discussion signals, format as: 'R{round}: [{from}] {type}: {summary}'
Color code: proposal → text-blue-400, opinion → text-gray-300, objection → text-orange-400, consensus → text-green-400
For consensus signals, append position: '(agree)', '(disagree)', or '(modify)'
Non-discussion signals keep existing formatting unchanged
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Even with the Discussion Thread panel, ActivityFeed may still show discussion signals in non-discussion layouts or when scrolling past.
